channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Lawmakers launch investigation into 23andMe after bankruptcy
item.description: A group of House lawmakers launched a probe Thursday into 23andMe's handling of customers' sensitive data following the company's filing for bankruptcy last month. In a letter to 23andMe, House Commerce and Energy Committee Chair Brett Guthrie (R-Ky.) and Reps. Gus Bilirakis (R-Fla.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Apple Fixes Two Zero-Days in iOS 18.4.1 Security Patch
item.description: Apple rushes out a security patch to fix two zero-day attacks being actively used, affecting iOS, Macs, iPads, and other Apple devices.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: What Strava buying Runna means for users of both fitness apps – according to their CEOs
item.description: "We're keeping the two apps separate for the foreseeable": Strava and Runna CEOs tell us their acquisition won't be like Fitbit and Google
item.dc:creator: matt.evans@futurenet.com (Matt Evans), TechRadar

item.title: CoreWeave offers cloud-based Grace Blackwell GPUs for AI training
item.description: Cloud services provider CoreWeave has announced it is offering Nvidia’s GB200 NVL72 systems, otherwise known as “Grace Blackwell,” to customers looking to do intensive AI training.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Insta360 teaser suggests it could launch world's best 360 camera soon – here are 5 things I want to see
item.description: Insta360 has posted a teaser for a new launch event on April 22, and rumors are pointing towards a new X5. Here's what we want to see from the 360 camera.
item.dc:creator: mark.wilson@futurenet.com (Mark Wilson), TechRadar

item.title: Google's digital ad network declared an illegal monopoly, joining its search engine in penalty box
item.description: Google has been branded an abusive monopolist by a federal judge for the second time in less than a year, this time for illegally exploiting some of its online marketing technology to boost the profits fueling an internet empire currently worth $1.8 trillion.
item.dc:creator: Michael Liedtke, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Federal judge rules Google has illegal ad tech monopoly
item.description: A federal judge ruled Thursday that Google illegally acquired and maintained a monopoly over advertising technology, the second time in less than a year that the tech giant has been found in violation of antitrust laws. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Salesforce CEO Marc Benioff: AI agents will be like Iron Man’s Jarvis
item.description: AI agents are more than a productivity boost; they’re fundamentally reshaping customer interactions and business operations.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: 7 simple ways to protect your credit cards while traveling
item.description: Kurt "CyberGuy" Knutsson shares several easy ways to keep your credit cards safe from digital thieves while you're traveling this summer.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A restaurant in Havana defies Cuba's tech struggles with digital menus and a robot waitress
item.description: HAVANA (AP) — For Sonia Pérez, stepping into Havana’s Doña Alicia restaurant felt like entering a movie.
item.dc:creator: ANDREA RODRÍGUEZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 10 most used gen AI tools in the enterprise
item.description: Generative AI has entered the enterprise in a big way since OpenAI first launched ChatGPT in 2022. According to Precedence Research, the global gen AI market was over $25 billion in 2024 and is forecast to reach a staggering $803 billion by 2033.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Trump's potential semiconductor tariffs spook tech industry
item.description: President Trump’s potential tariffs on semiconductors are stoking alarm within the technology sector as companies brace for ripple effects across the industry and its competitive standing on a global stage.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Headlamp: A multicluster management UI for Kubernetes
item.description: As important as Kubernetes is to the modern cloud-native stack, it’s still not easy to use on your own hardware. That’s surprising, considering how long it’s been around.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Enter the parallel universe of Java’s Vector API
item.description: If there is one thing you can describe as an obsession for both developers and devops, it’s how to improve the performance of applications.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Proposed rule change on endangered species triggers alarm for environmentalists
item.description: The Trump administration plans to rewrite part of the Endangered Species Act that prohibits harming the habitats of endangered and threatened species.
item.dc:creator: Tammy Webber, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg wraps up testimony in antitrust case
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg emphasized in federal court on Wednesday that he bought Instagram and WhatsApp because he saw value in the companies — not to take out competitors, as the Federal Trade Commission alleges in a historic antitrust trial.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Tech shares fall after Nvidia says new US controls on exports of AI chip will cost it $5.5 billion
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Shares in computer chipmakers slumped Wednesday after Nvidia said tighter U.S. government controls on exports of computer chips used for artificial intelligence will cost it an extra $5.5 billion.
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korean full-stack AI startup Upstage eyes expansion in the US and Japan
item.description: South Korean AI startup Upstage is ramping up global expansion in the US and Japan, positioning its full-stack AI platform to serve enterprise clients.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Spotify down for thousands of users
item.description: Music streaming service Spotify was down for hours on Wednesday for tens of thousands of users. The outage began around Wednesday morning, with more than 48,600 users reporting issues around the same time at 9:40 a.m. EDT, according to outage tracking site Downdetector.com.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Don't like a columnist's opinion? Los Angeles Times offers an AI-generated opposing viewpoint
item.description: In a colorful commentary for the Los Angeles Times, Matt K. Lewis argued that callousness is a central feature of the second Trump administration, particularly its policies of deportation and bureaucratic cutbacks.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Meta CEO Zuckerberg considered spinning off Instagram in 2018 over antitrust worries, email says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg once considered separating Instagram from its parent company due to worries about antitrust litigation, according to an email shown Tuesday on the second day of an antitrust trial alleging Meta illegally monopolized the social media market.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: China pursuing 3 alleged US operatives over cyberattacks during Asian Games
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — China said Tuesday it is pursuing three alleged U.S. operatives accused of carrying out cyberattacks on Chinese infrastructure during the Asian Games held in the city of Harbin in February. A notice from the Harbin police headquarters named them as Katheryn A. Wilson, Robert J.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER BODEEN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: The network blueprint to take your modern enterprise global
item.description: Adopting new technologies is synonymous with improving productivity, enhancing decision-making, and driving innovations today. Almost every enterprise integrates some form of AI/ML capabilities within their operations or tapping into the Internet of Things (IoT) to keep operations at the edge.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg takes the stand in historic antitrust trial
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg took to the witness stand on the first day of a historic antitrust trial to defend his company against allegations it illegally monopolized the social media market.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Intel sells majority stake in Altera to Silver Lake investors in a deal valued at $8.75 billion
item.description: Intel announced Monday that it was selling the majority of its Altera chip business to technology investor Silver Lake in a deal valued at $8.75 billion.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Nvidia plans to manufacture AI chips in the US for the first time
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Nvidia announced Monday that it will produce its artificial intelligence super computers in the United States for the first time.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Meta says it will resume AI training with public content from European users
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Social media company Meta said Monday that it will start using publicly available content from European users to train its artificial intelligence models, resuming work put on hold last year after activists raised concerns about data privacy.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: As legacy of Ford fades in Brazil, China's BYD looks to extend global lead in EV sales
item.description: CAMACARI, Brazil (AP) — When Chinese automaker BYD Co. announced plans to build a massive factory in the hardscrabble city of Camacari, in Brazil’s northeastern Bahia state, locals saw a new beginning. After years of economic stagnation following Ford Motor Co.
item.dc:creator: FABIANO MAISONNAVE, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Meta vs. Federal Trade Commission trial begins Monday. What to know
item.description: The FTC alleges Meta violated competition laws when purchasing Instagram and WhatsApp, in 2012 and 2014 respectively.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Some top tech leaders have embraced Trump. That's created a political divide in Silicon Valley
item.description: SAN JOSE, Calif. (AP) — Like many in the tech industry, Jeremy Lyons used to think of himself as a relatively apolitical guy.
item.dc:creator: NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Japanese leader says he hopes Osaka expo will help reunite a divided world
item.description: OSAKA, Japan (AP) — Japanese Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba, speaking at Saturday's opening ceremony for the Osaka expo, expressed his hope that the event will help restore global unity in a world plagued by conflicts and trade wars.
item.dc:creator: AYAKA McGILL and MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, ABC 4
